disambiguation_rules.the department of state: explicitly named in SEC. 6(a)(1) as the primary reporting entity
disambiguation_rules.aukus partners: contextually refers to Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States as trilateral partners in the agreement
disambiguation_rules.national disclosure policy committee: Explicitly named coordinating entity in SEC. 12(a)(1)
disambiguation_rules.arms transfer and technology release sen: Explicitly named coordinating entity in SEC. 12(a)(1)
disambiguation_rules.government of canada: New entity added—SEC. 12 expands AUKUS-adjacent framework to include Canada alongside Australia and UK

parsing_rules: Technology release policies should capture both Pillar designations (Pillar One/Two) as distinct named initiatives with associated capability categories (e.g., submarine systems, hypersonic missiles, AI, quantum, cyber, undersea).

oddities.note: Document specifies reporting requirements across eight trilateral Lines of Effort (undersea capabilities, quantum technologies, AI and autonomy, advanced cyber capabilities) with only four listed in the provided text—full enumeration may appear in complete document.

This section expands recipient countries to include Canada, distinct from the AUKUS trilateral framework (Australia, UK, US) documented in community context. The section treats all three countries identically in FMS preclearance and anticipatory release policy, suggesting a broader allied technology partnership framework.
